title: Fleshly Roasted German Pepper Gratin
description: A grassy, shattering, kid-friendly German dish featuring Romano peppers, perfect for a weeknight dinner. Simply broil and serve over slate-tile roasted potatoes.
instructions: Toss the sliced potatoes with a little bit of butter and season with salt and pepper.
Arrange the potatoes in a single layer on a slate-tile baking sheet.
Broil the potatoes for 5 minutes, or until they start to brown.
Meanwhile, sauté the onion and garlic in butter until softened.
Add the Romano peppers and cook until tender.
Stir in the flour and cook for 1 minute.
Add the gruyere cheese and heavy cream, stirring until the cheese is melted and the sauce is smooth.
Season the sauce with salt and pepper to taste.
To assemble the gratin, spread half of the potato slices in the bottom of a baking dish.
Top the potatoes with the Romano pepper and cheese sauce, then top with the remaining potato slices.
Broil the gratin for 5-7 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the sauce is bubbly.
